articles.summary: Can we finally treat joint pain without trading relief for risk? AlzeCure’s new ACD137 data hints at a shift in osteoarthritis care. What if treating chronic joint pain didn’t require choosing between effectiveness and long-term harm? articles.summary: As body composition scans move into the longevity mainstream, Fitnescity Health wants consumers to look beyond the cheapest option. DEXA scans sat in the background of clinical medicine for years, mostly associated with bone density tests for older adults. Now, they are becoming a defining tool in the longevity conversation. articles.title: Alzheimer’s gene linked to neuronal DNA resilience
articles.summary: Buck Institute researchers link APOE2 longevity protection to DNA repair and neuronal senescence resistance. Scientists have long known that carriers of the APOE2 variant appear more likely to reach advanced […] The post Alzheimer’s gene linked to neuronal DNA resilience appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Resolution Therapeutics shares positive preclinical data for liver disease cell therapy
articles.summary: Resolution Therapeutics will present new preclinical data on its engineered regenerative macrophage therapy, RTX001, in an oral presentation at the ASGCT Annual Meeting in Boston. According to the company, the data demonstrate RTX001’s pharmacology and pharmacokinetic profile, safety and tolerability in mouse models, and efficacy at reducing liver enzymes, inflammation, and fibrosis. The company claims […] The post Resolution Therapeutics shares positive preclinical data for liver disease cell therapy appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: NeuroVision acquires Durin Life Sciences to expand diagnostics
articles.summary: NeuroVision announced the acquisition of Durin Life Sciences, combining Durin’s Duritect blood-based diagnostic tests with NeuroVision’s retinal imaging technology and its BrainHealth.net telehealth patient engagement platform. The combined enterprise will operate under the NeuroVision name and remain led by Steven Verdooner, co-founder and CEO, with Durin majority owner Ric Edelman joining the board. articles.title: HCW Biologics drug prevents rare lung disease in preclinical studies
articles.summary: HCW Biologics reported that HCW11-040, a second-generation pembrolizumab-based fusion immunotherapeutic, prevented bronchopulmonary dysplasia in a clinically relevant and highly stringent animal model during IND-enabling studies conducted with Queen’s University at Kingston, Ontario. According to the company, HCW11-040 is designed to block checkpoint receptors and engage costimulatory receptors to activate exhausted immune cells and eliminate high […] The post HCW Biologics drug prevents rare lung disease in preclinical studies appeared first on Longevity.Technology .

articles.title: Student-built system unlocks fully autonomous electroporation for 96- and 384-well workflows
articles.summary: Inside the California NanoSystems Institute at UCLA, where some of the most advanced and technical automated infrastructure on campus resides, two students saw an opportunity hiding in plain sight.

articles.title: Single-molecule RNA mapping may reveal how shape shifts steer health and disease
articles.summary: Researchers from A*STAR Genome Institute of Singapore (A*STAR GIS) have developed a new method to study individual RNA molecules and reveal how their structures influence gene regulation, a fundamental process that affects how cells function in health and disease. Their work was published in Nature Methods.

articles.title: Implantable bacteria can now be safely contained, clearing a major hurdle for fighting infection and cancer
articles.summary: Researchers have long known that bacteria could potentially be used to deliver therapeutic drugs inside the human body. However, safely and successfully carrying out such a feat in humans has been a challenge. But now, researchers from Harvard have made another step forward toward the goal of using microbes as medicine. Their recent study, published in Science, details a novel method for containing engineered bacteria to keep them from infecting their host while still successfully delivering potentially life-saving medications.

articles.title: Bacterial energy enzyme reveals dual-trigger sodium pump mechanism, offering antibiotic clues
articles.summary: The Na+-NQR enzyme is vital for energy production in pathogenic bacteria like the one that causes cholera, making it a highly promising target for new antibiotics. Researchers combined modified artificial intelligence techniques with extensive supercomputer simulations to visualize the hidden, dynamic movements of this enzyme during sodium transport.

articles.title: Experimentally validated AI model predicts virulence of tomato yellow leaf curl virus
articles.summary: A CBBL research team led by Professor Balachandran Manavalan from the Department of Integrative Biotechnology at Sungkyunkwan University has developed DeepTYLCV, an accurate and interpretable artificial intelligence model for predicting the virulence of tomato yellow leaf curl virus (TYLCV).

articles.title: Discovery could reshape RNA editing with DNA-guided CRISPR
articles.summary: A team of engineers at the University of Florida has developed a new form of CRISPR technology that could make diagnostics and treatments safer, more precise, and more affordable, while opening the door to entirely new ways of controlling disease.

articles.title: Guide DNA — not RNA — expands the CRISPR toolkit
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 15 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03138-9 Cas12 nucleases can use guide DNA instead of guide RNA, which switches their targets from DNA to RNA.

articles.title: DNA-guided CRISPR–Cas12 for cellular RNA targeting
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 15 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03129-w ΨDNA guides are used with Cas12 for precise, programmable targeting of cellular RNA.

articles.title: Widespread DNA off-targeting confounds RNA chromatin occupancy studies
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 15 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03130-3 Many long noncoding RNA–DNA binding peaks detected using common assays arise from technical artifacts.

articles.title: Engineered proteins store digital files with 30 times density at one-tenth cost
articles.summary: Massive volumes of digital data are generated every day from AI training, big data analytics and smart devices. As conventional hard drives and cloud storage are increasingly constrained by high costs, limited capacity, high power consumption and short lifespans, molecular data storage has emerged as a breakthrough storage alternative.

articles.title: Strategy to repurchase $1.5B of 2029 convertible notes
articles.summary: The notes carry a 0% coupon, but can be converted into equity in the company if holders choose to redeem their notes for shares.

articles.title: Harvard dumps ether ETF as Abu Dhabi sovereign fund keeps adding to bitcoin positions
articles.summary: Abu Dhabi-based Mubadala added over $90 million to its already sizeable position in BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF.

articles.title: Bitcoin analysis sees 'bear trap' as BTC price passes two-week lows under $78K
articles.summary: Bitcoin fell below $78,000 for the first time since the start of May, but traders refused to give up hope of a BTC price rebound coming next.

articles.title: OpenAI partners with Malta to give all citizens free ChatGPT Plus access
articles.summary: In a world-first deal, OpenAI and Malta will give all citizens free ChatGPT Plus for a year after they complete a government-backed AI literacy course.

articles.title: Crypto users are choosing juicy yields over protection, putting billions at risk of hacks
articles.summary: DeFi insurance protocols debuted with huge ambitions during the 2020 crypto boom. But as hacks evolved and users chased yields over protection, most of the sector collapsed under the same risks it was built to cover.

articles.title: The $293 million KelpDAO hack shows why DeFi is finally being forced to grow up
articles.summary: For protocol founders and security researchers, the incident reinforced a broader shift underway across crypto: DeFi is no longer primarily battling coding bugs. It’s battling complexity.

articles.summary: The token jumped 5% after a Senate committee moved the market-structure bill forward, reviving hopes that legal clarity can pull deeper institutional money into XRP products.

articles.title: THORChain confirms $10M exploit, rolls out recovery portal for affected users
articles.summary: THORChain has launched a recovery portal following a $10 million exploit, allowing affected users across four chains to revoke malicious approvals and claim refunds.
